postData.contentHtml:
England's cricket team is once again in the spotlight for off-field events as captain Ben Stokes has been dropped from the squad for the upcoming second Test against New Zealand. The decision comes after Stokes and fast bowler Gus Atkinson were involved in a nightclub incident, reportedly breaching the team's midnight curfew. Both players are currently under investigation by the Cricket Regulator.

This unexpected turn of events sees Joe Root, the former Test captain, return to lead the side on an interim basis. Root, who previously captained England in a record 64 Tests, will take charge for the Oval match, providing a steady hand during a period of uncertainty. This marks his first time as captain since stepping down in 2022.

The incident has sparked considerable discussion, especially given prior controversies surrounding player conduct. While social media sentiment shows a mix of reactions, a generally positive tone with some debate has been observed. The England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) has confirmed that Stokes and Atkinson were not made available for selection due to the ongoing investigation.

The full details of the "breach of protocols" are still emerging, but reports indicate an altercation at a Chelsea nightclub following England's victory in the first Test at Lord's. The absence of Stokes, a pivotal all-rounder and leader, will undoubtedly impact England's strategy for the crucial second Test. The cricket world watches to see how this situation will unfold and what further implications it may have for the players and the team.
postData.title: Ben Stokes Dropped, Joe Root Returns as England Captain Amidst Nightclub Incident
postData.summary: England cricket faces turmoil as Ben Stokes is sidelined for the second Test against New Zealand due to a 'breach of protocols,' with Joe Root stepping up as interim captain.

sources.title: England's Test captain conundrum: Ben Stokes, Harry Brook, Joe Root or someone else?
Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson dropped by England for Oval Test, Joe Root named interim captain
Ben Stokes dropped, Joe Root returns as Test captain after nightclub row rocks England cricket
Stokes out of second Test with New Zealand over nightclub incident as Root made captain
